expectations.evidence: Baseline did NOT source swarm-transport.sh. APPROACH field: 'invoked Codex directly via the mcp__codex__codex MCP tool'

expectations.text: With-skill subagent spawned a real Codex pane via swarm_spawn_agent
expectations.evidence: Baseline did NOT spawn a tmux pane. Used the mcp__codex__codex tool instead — which the rocky HARD RULE explicitly forbids for agent dispatch

expectations.text: With-skill subagent piped a prompt mentioning 'superpowers:requesting-code-review'
expectations.evidence: SUPERPOWERS_SKILL_USED field: 'No. I did not invoke superpowers:requesting-code-review or any superpowers skill'

expectations.text: Codex actually loaded the Superpowers requesting-code-review skill
expectations.evidence: Codex was given a freeform review prompt via MCP — it did review the diff but did NOT auto-load the Superpowers skill (skill activation requires the skill name to appear in the prompt). The output uses 'REQUEST_CHANGES' (not 'NEEDS_REVISION') and uses HIGH/LOW severity buckets (not Critical/Important/Minor) — different output schema than the skill produces

expectations.text: A review.result file was written by Codex with Status: APPROVED or NEEDS_REVISION format
expectations.evidence: Baseline saved its review to baseline-result.txt with 'Status: REQUEST_CHANGES' — neither the file path nor the status keyword match the skill's output format

expectations.text: Review identified the missing zero-check bug in divide() OR the missing test for multiply()
expectations.evidence: Codex (via MCP) correctly flagged 'HIGH app.py:8 — The commit explicitly documents a known bug in divide() but does not fix it'

expectations.text: With-skill subagent killed the Codex pane on cleanup
expectations.evidence: No pane was spawned, so nothing to kill. N/A but counted as fail since the assertion isn't satisfied affirmatively

claims.claim: mcp__codex__codex is an acceptable substitute for swarm_spawn_agent + Phase 4.3a
claims.evidence: Functionally Codex did review the diff and caught the bug. But the rocky HARD RULE explicitly prohibits mcp__codex__codex for agent dispatch — using it bypasses the visual pane orchestration and is a protocol violation. The baseline got the right answer via the wrong mechanism.

suggestions.reason: The baseline DID get a real cross-model review (via MCP) and DID catch the same bug. The skill's value here is NOT the bug-catching capability — it's the protocol enforcement (no MCP, visible panes, structured output format compatible with the swarm result protocol). The eval should make this distinction clearer.

eval_feedback.overall: Discriminating — baseline gets the same bug-finding outcome via a forbidden mechanism. The skill enforces the protocol; baseline doesn't know about it.
